metadata.title: Leetgrind Operational Packets
metadata.purpose: Define governed execution mode for autonomous and multi-agent work.

assumptions.assumption: The product remains local-first unless the user explicitly changes direction.
The workspace uses TypeScript strict mode.
AI providers are optional runtime integrations and must be replaceable.

retry-budget.rule: Retry a failing verification command at most once after a targeted fix.
Do not keep changing unrelated files to make a broad build pass.
Escalate to the user if the failure requires product or architecture direction.

stop-conditions.condition: The task contradicts Product Vision or requirements.
The implementation would break local-first assumptions.
A package dependency cycle is required to proceed.
User code execution cannot be sandboxed or bounded.
Provider credentials, auth flow, or legal boundary is unavailable or unclear.
GRACE artifacts disagree on a decision that materially affects implementation.

required-steps.step: Read AGENTS.md and relevant GRACE artifacts.
Update requirements or development-plan if the feature changes scope.
Implement only within owned files unless a documented dependency requires expansion.
Run module-specific verification and global verification as required.
Report changed files, verification evidence, and residual risks.
Prove the refactor is scoped and necessary.
Update graph or contracts if boundaries move.
Keep public behavior unchanged unless explicitly requested.
Assign each worker a module and owned file set.
Prevent workers from editing shared contracts in parallel unless one worker owns the contract update.
Collect worker outputs and changed paths.
Fix root cause without broad rewrite.
Add or update verification when the bug could recur.

packet.name: Base workspace bootstrap
packet.result: Workspace, dependencies, baseline packages, web app, and local server were initialized.

packet.name: GRACE methodology bootstrap
packet.result: Project now has agent-facing contracts, module graph, verification plan, and execution packet templates.

packet.name: Local data and API backbone
packet.expected-output: Core schema, repositories, database context, and typed local API exist for profile, goals, skills, documents, attempts, evaluations, evidence, recommendations, and reviews.
packet.result: Local PGLite schema, Drizzle migration, repositories, server app context, tRPC routers, and minimal web health status wiring were implemented.

packet.name: User profile, goals, and onboarding
packet.expected-output: User can complete onboarding, define multiple goals, self-assess skills, set Russian or English UI language preference, set programming language preferences, and store resume context locally.
packet.result: User profile, goals, skill self-assessment, resume context, UI language preference, programming language preference, draft saving, onboarding completion, localized onboarding UI, dashboard state, and verification coverage were implemented.

packet.name: Progress dashboard and knowledge graph
packet.expected-output: Dashboard shows goal readiness, strengths, weaknesses, next actions, recent activity, and an interactive skill graph.
packet.result: Dashboard read models, deterministic recommendations, idempotent common skill graph templates, tRPC procedures, Cytoscape graph preview, goal detail, skill detail, history route, and localized UI copy were implemented.

packet.name: AI providers, agents, and RAG
packet.expected-output: User can configure providers through settings, keep secrets in the OS keychain, run validated OpenRouter calls, ingest local documents, retrieve cited content context, and store agent run traces.
packet.result: OpenRouter-first provider registry, keychain-backed credential storage, provider settings UI, provider test flow, document chunk ingestion and retrieval, typed mentor preview runs, localized settings routes, and agent run tracing were implemented.

packet.name: Assessments, lessons, and recommendations
packet.expected-output: User can take assessments, receive evaluations, store evidence, view generated lessons, and see explainable recommendations.
packet.result: Phase 05 now has dedicated assessment persistence, validated workflows, lesson storage, recommendation actions, localized web routes, and verification coverage.

packet.name: Coding practice IDE and runner
packet.expected-output: User can solve one-language coding tasks in a web editor, run deterministic tests, request hints, submit, and receive AI review.
packet.result: Initial JavaScript runner abstraction now normalizes test results, captures stdout and stderr, enforces per-test timeout, blocks dynamic code generation, and reports unsupported languages explicitly.

packet.name: Spaced repetition, history, and mock interviews
packet.expected-output: User can review due knowledge, inspect progress history, run mock interviews, and receive readiness reports.

packet.name: Desktop readiness, hardening, and polish
packet.expected-output: App has data export/import, diagnostics, safer code execution boundaries, provider reliability UX, and a documented Tauri path.

packet.name: Frontend design system foundation
packet.expected-output: Frontend work has a documented design contract, centralized Mantine theme tokens, local font assets, and desktop/mobile browser verification.
packet.result: Design contract, theme tokens, local typography assets, AGENTS guidance, and Playwright design quality checks were added.
